factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Volume declined in almost every market segment due to the deteriorating economic conditions brought on by the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to the COVID-19 pandemic, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Pricing power is holding, with core pricing gains offsetting some declines.

evidence.text: Average revenue per car (ARC) was down 6% due to negative mix of traffic and lower fuel surcharge revenue, partially offset by core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is holding, but there are mixed effects from declines and gains.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments, which is harmful.

evidence.text: Freight revenue from bulk shipments decreased in the second quarter of 2020 compared to 2019 due to a 15% volume decline, lower fuel surcharge revenue, and negative business mix, partially offset by core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments, which is harmful.

evidence.text: Volume declined in almost every market segment due to the deteriorating economic conditions brought on by the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining significantly due to the pandemic.

factors.summary: End markets are mixed, with automotive and industrial chemicals declining, while intermodal improved.

evidence.text: The most notable impact on our revenue was the temporary suspension of automotive production and the corollary effect it had on products used for auto manufacturing. [...] Finally, low crude oil and natural gas prices continue to negatively impact our shipments of coal, petroleum products and sand. [...] Trans-Pacific volumes improved sequentially throughout the quarter.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End markets are mixed with some sectors declining and others improving.

factors.summary: Input costs are declining, particularly fuel prices.

evidence.text: Freight revenues from fuel surcharge programs were $206 million in the second quarter of 2020 compared to $399 million in the same period of 2019. The decline was driven by lower fuel prices and reduced volume, partially offset by the lag impact on fuel surcharge recovery.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are declining, particularly fuel prices, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through furloughs and salary cuts.

evidence.text: We implemented a temporary unpaid leave of absence for management and administrative employees, decreased executive and Board of Director salaries by 25% for the same period, and temporarily closed three of our repair facilities.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are being managed effectively through furloughs and salary cuts.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to revenue declines outpacing cost savings.

evidence.text: These reductions were not enough to offset the revenue declines as operating income decreased 27% in the second quarter compared to the same period in 2019.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure due to revenue declines outpacing cost savings.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Cost savings from lower volume, productivity, and lower fuel prices drove operating expenses down 22% from 2019.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from cost reduction actions indicate improvement.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through operational transformation.

evidence.text: Despite the adversity from the COVID-19 pandemic throughout the quarter, we continued our operational transformation, largely mitigating the impact of the volume loss, while our employees improved the safety of the railroad and provided our customers an uninterrupted, enhanced service product.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational transformation successfully mitigated pandemic impacts and improved service.

factors.summary: Supply chain is still disrupted, particularly in intermodal.

evidence.text: COVID-19 also disrupted supply chains between Asia and the United States driving declines in intermodal shipments.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: COVID-19 disrupted supply chains, particularly in intermodal.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is stable with ample capacity for growth.

evidence.text: As volume increases, we are returning some locomotives and freight cars to service along with recalling employees from furlough, and we have ample capacity for further growth.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable utilization with capacity for growth shows preparedness.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed and are not excessive.

evidence.text: We have been, and we expect to continue to be, in compliance with our debt covenants.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Managed inventory without excess indicates stable management.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring through furloughs and layoffs.

evidence.text: We implemented a temporary unpaid leave of absence for management and administrative employees, decreased executive and Board of Director salaries by 25% for the same period, and temporarily closed three of our repair facilities.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Furloughs and layoffs indicate workforce reduction.

evidence.text: Enhanced safety procedures have been implemented across the system and adjusted based on the local working environment, along with new procedures and policies as our employees transition back into our office buildings.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Enhanced safety and new procedures support stable relations.

factors.summary: Management is cautious with a focus on cost management and productivity.

evidence.text: Ultimately, we will continue to focus on what we can control, such as managing costs, increasing productivity, and seeking new business opportunities, as well as, protecting our employees, customers and communities and providing excellent service to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on cost management and productivity shows constructive approach.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the potential resurgence of COVID-19 and economic uncertainty.

evidence.text: While we are seeing the industrial sector begin to rebound during the third quarter, the resurgence of COVID-19 and how governments and consumers react could result in customer disruptions or an elongated recovery period.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The potential resurgence of COVID-19 and economic uncertainty pose significant risks, indicating a clear deterioration.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include economic conditions and fuel prices.

evidence.text: The varying levels of action across different industries in an attempt to slow the spread had a significant impact on the demand to ship freight. [...] Low crude oil and natural gas prices continue to negatively impact our shipments of coal, petroleum products and sand.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Low fuel prices negatively impacting shipments and economic conditions present moderate headwinds.
